title: State Small Business Credit Initiative (SSBCI)
summary: The State Small Business Credit Initiative (SSBCI) is a nearly $10 billion federal program reauthorized and expanded by the American Rescue Plan Act to support small businesses through state-administered capital programs including equity/venture capital, loan participation, loan guarantees, and collateral support. The Treasury page serves as a program information portal rather than a market-moving announcement.
key_points: SSBCI is a nearly $10 billion program to support small businesses and entrepreneurship across the United States
Reauthorized and expanded by the American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA)
Provides funds to states, DC, territories, and Tribal governments to create tailored programs for small businesses
Expected to catalyze up to $10 of private investment for every $1 of SSBCI Capital Program funding
Offers funding through equity/venture capital, loan participation, loan guarantee, collateral support, and capital access programs
Page contains links to annual reports, FAQs, and program contact information
why_it_matters: This source is a program information portal describing an existing federal credit initiative. It does not contain new policy announcements, rate decisions, or economic data that would affect market expectations. The SSBCI page provides no actionable information for financial market participants beyond general program structure.
macro_context: SSBCI represents a fiscal transfer to small businesses (~$10B), but this Treasury webpage does not announce new policy measures or provide economic data. It is informational content about an existing program structure. The TARGET classification as 'macro catalysts' appears mismatched with the source content.
important_numbers: $10 private investment per $1 SSBCI funding (expected leverage ratio)
